CREATE TABLE test_strings (s1 VARCHAR(100), s2 VARCHAR(100));
SELECT startswith(s1, s2, true) FROM test_strings;
SELECT startswith(s1, s2, false) FROM test_strings;
SELECT s1, s2, startswith(s1, s2, true) AS case_insensitive, startswith(s1, s2, false) AS case_sensitive FROM test_strings;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE startswith(s1, s2, true);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE startswith(s1, s2, false);
SELECT DISTINCT s1 FROM test_strings WHERE startswith(s1, 'b', true) AND s1 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT s1 FROM test_strings WHERE s2 IN (SELECT s2 FROM test_strings WHERE startswith(s1, s2, true));
SELECT a.s FROM additional_data a JOIN test_strings b ON startswith(a.s, b.s2, true) WHERE b.s2 IS NOT NULL;
